name: Twenty
one_liner: Twenty is an open-source, customizable CRM platform that provides an API-first architecture for managing customer data and building internal business applications.
access_notes: Developers can generate API keys directly within their Twenty workspace settings for both cloud and self-hosted instances.
auth_notes: Uses Bearer token authentication via API keys generated in workspace settings; OAuth2 is also supported for external applications.

evidence.claim: Twenty provides REST and GraphQL APIs generated from the workspace schema.
Search citation: Twenty | #1 Open Source CRM
Search citation: Twenty: Free Open Source CRM
Search citation: My Experience With Modern Open Source CRM: Twenty CRM - DEV Community

api_notes: The API is schema-driven, meaning all core entities (People, Companies, etc.) and custom objects created by the user are automatically accessible via REST and GraphQL endpoints. Metadata API is also available for programmatic schema management.
mcp_notes: No official or well-known community Model Context Protocol server was found in the official documentation.
